pattern: Extending a ring buffer with operations at both ends lets a fixed-size deque insert and delete from front or rear in constant time by moving head and tail indices modulo the capacity.
hints: Reuse the circular-buffer idea but allow growth from both ends; you still only need an array of size k, a head index, and a count.
Inserting at the front means stepping the head backward (with wrap), while inserting at the rear writes just past the last element.

Keep head and count. insertLast writes at (head + count) % k; insertFront moves head to (head - 1 + k) % k and writes there; deleteFront advances head; deleteLast just drops the count; both reject when full/empty.

edge_inputs.description: mix front/rear inserts, overflow, peek, delete, then reuse
capacity one supports both insert directions but holds at most one item
peeks on empty return -1 and deletes on empty fail
front and rear inserts must wrap past both ends of the array consistently

stress.description: long randomized mix of all eight operations against a small capacity
stress.note: small capacity and both-end operations force frequent wrap-around and boundary checks
